insights.title: Global voice start modulator returns wrong value when note number is remapped in a MIDI processor
insights.summary: If a MIDI script blocks the incoming note and plays a different note number (e.g., always plays middle C), the global velocity modulator in the target synth only responds correctly when the played note matches the redirected note. The value lookup is keyed by note number at the time the global container pre-processes the event, so the artificially-generated note number is used for the lookup instead of the original played note.
insights.verify_hint: See GlobalModulators.cpp calculateVoiceStartValue: lookup uses getConnectedContainer()->getConstantVoiceValue(connectedMod, noteNumber) where noteNumber comes from the HiseEvent passed to the consumer, not the original incoming MIDI event

insights.title: Global voice start modulators do not work with notes created via Synth.playNote()
insights.summary: Notes generated by Synth.playNote() are created after the global modulator has already processed the original event. The new artificial note is assigned a new event ID, and no voice-start value is stored for it in the container's 128-element array at that note number. The global modulator will produce silence or undefined output for these notes.

insights.title: Use Message.setTransposeAmount() instead of Synth.playNote() to keep global modulators working
insights.summary: When the goal is to redirect an incoming note to a different pitch, use Message.setTransposeAmount() rather than blocking the original event and calling Synth.playNote(). This preserves the original event ID, so the global modulator container can correctly associate the voice-start value with the note. This also avoids stuck-note problems that arise from mismatched note-on/off pairs.

insights.title: Replace global velocity modulator with a script voice start modulator when using artificial notes
insights.summary: When notes are generated programmatically (arpeggios, strum scripts, chord generators), replace the GlobalVoiceStartModulator with a Script Voice Start Modulator. Reimplement the velocity curve logic inside the script (typically two lines: read velocity via Message.getVelocity(), scale through a table). Always end the callback with return 1.0 as a safe fallback.

insights.title: Missing return statement in script voice start modulator silences all voices
insights.summary: If the script callback has a conditional branch that does not explicitly return a value, the missing return is converted to 0.0, which mutes all notes that hit that branch. Always ensure every code path returns a float value; use return 1.0 as the default fallback at the end of the function.

insights.title: Global velocity mod combined with an arpeggiator causes note dropouts
insights.summary: When a global velocity modulator is active alongside an arpeggiator, some notes drop out or play silently. The arpeggiator generates artificial events that lack a stored voice-start value in the global container. Workarounds: disable velocity modulation when the arp is enabled, or use a local velocity modulator directly on the sampler instead of a global one.
insights.verify_hint: Check GlobalModulatorContainer preStartVoice: confirm whether artificially generated arp notes trigger preStartVoice for velocity storage

insights.title: Synth.getModulator() cannot find modulators that are higher in the signal chain
insights.summary: Calling Synth.getModulator() from a script processor cannot locate a GlobalVoiceStartModulator (or its container) that sits above the script in the module hierarchy. The lookup is scoped to the same synth or below.
insights.verify_hint: Check ScriptingApi::Synth::getModulator implementation for search scope; grep ProcessorHelpers::getFirstProcessorWithName

insights.title: Use a MidiList to pass real velocity to a script voice start modulator when note numbers are remapped
insights.summary: When multiple notes are remapped or chord notes are generated from a single key press, record the incoming velocity per note number into a MidiList on note-on, then read it back inside the script voice start modulator with midiList.getValue(Message.getNoteNumber()) / 127.0. This correctly handles polyphonic cases where the velocity must follow the original played key.
